root_text.an4.238:1.2: idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sabyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti …pe… idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kaṇhaṁ kaṇhavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.238:2.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco abyābajjhaṁ kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti …pe… idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ sukkaṁ sukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.238:3.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ekacco sabyābajjhampi abyābajjhampi kāyasaṅkhāraṁ abhisaṅkharoti …pe… idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ kaṇhasukkaṁ kaṇhasukkavipākaṁ.
root_text.an4.238:4.1: Katamañca, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati?
root_text.an4.238:4.2: Satisambojjhaṅgo, dhammavicayasambojjhaṅgo, vīriyasambojjhaṅgo, pītisambojjhaṅgo, passaddhisambojjhaṅgo, samādhisambojjhaṅgo, upekkhāsambojjhaṅgo—
root_text.an4.238:4.3: idaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, kammaṁ akaṇhaasukkaṁ akaṇhaasukkavipākaṁ kammakkhayāya saṁvattati.
root_text.an4.238:4.4: Imāni kho, bhikkhave, cattāri kammāni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditānī”ti.

translation_text.an4.238:1.1: “Mendicants, I declare these four kinds of deeds, having realized them with my own insight….
translation_text.an4.238:1.2: And what are dark deeds with dark results? It’s when someone makes hurtful choices by way of body, speech, and mind. These are called dark deeds with dark results.
translation_text.an4.238:2.1: And what are bright deeds with bright results?
translation_text.an4.238:2.2: It’s when someone makes pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind. These are called bright deeds with bright results.
translation_text.an4.238:3.1: And what are dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results?
translation_text.an4.238:3.2: It’s when someone makes both hurtful and pleasing choices by way of body, speech, and mind. These are called dark and bright deeds with dark and bright results.
translation_text.an4.238:4.1: And what are neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds?
translation_text.an4.238:4.2: The awakening factors of mindfulness, investigation of principles, energy, rapture, tranquility, immersion, and equanimity.
translation_text.an4.238:4.3: These are called neither dark nor bright deeds with neither dark nor bright results, which lead to the ending of deeds.
translation_text.an4.238:4.4: These are the four kinds of deeds that I declare, having realized them with my own insight.”
